title: send_payment returns Err on MarkSpentFailed; txid is buried in error string, not machine-readable
description: payment.rs send_payment: when broadcast succeeds but mark_notes_spent fails, the function returns Err(SendPaymentError::MarkSpentFailed(msg)) where msg is a string containing the txid. The payment is on-chain and cannot be recalled. The caller has no way to extract the txid programmatically from the error variant — it is embedded in a human-readable string. Callers that need to display or record the confirmed txid (e.g. to show the user 'your payment was sent, txid=...') must parse the error string, which is fragile. MarkSpentFailed should carry the txid as a separate field: MarkSpentFailed { txid: String, detail: String }.
